title: Your First Conversation — Part 2
description: You meet Marek again. This time he wants to know more about you and your family.
context: You run into Marek again. He remembers you and wants to get to know you better. Use what you know.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks your name. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Volám sa Kai. — perfect introduction. Volám sa is the standard way to give your name.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Volám sa + your name is the correct response when someone asks for your name.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Nice to meet you! Where are you from?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks where you are from. Pick the country that is true for you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Som z... — correct pattern, any country works. The grammar is what matters here.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som z... + country is the correct pattern for saying where you're from.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Zaujímavé! A ako vyzeráte? Som zlý v opisovaní ľudí.
exchanges.translation: Interesting! And what do you look like? I'm bad at describing people.
exchanges.prompt: Marek jokes that he's bad with descriptions. Pick the description that is true for you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct grammar — Slovak adjectives change ending based on gender. Pick whichever describes you.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som vysoký/vysoká or Som nízky/nízka — pick the form that matches you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Aha, rozumiem. A aké máte vlasy?
exchanges.translation: Ah, I understand. And what color is your hair?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about your hair color. Pick the color that is true for you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám + color + vlasy. Correct pattern — combining a color word with a body part word.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Mám [color] vlasy is the pattern. Pick whichever color matches you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Dobre, teraz si vás predstavím lepšie! A máte tu rodinu?
exchanges.translation: Good, now I can picture you better! And do you have family here?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you have family in Slovakia. Pick whichever is true for you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám / Nemám + rodinu — correct grammar either way. Mám = I have, Nemám = I don't have.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Mám tu rodinu or Nemám tu rodinu — both are grammatically correct, pick what's true for you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Chápem. A máte súrodencov? Brata alebo sestru?
exchanges.translation: I understand. Do you have siblings? A brother or a sister?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you have siblings. Pick whichever is true for you.

choices.translation: I have a brother and a sister.

exchanges.correctFeedback: All correct grammar. Mám = I have, Nemám = I don't have, súrodenci = siblings.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Mám/Nemám + brata/sestru/súrodencov — pick whichever is true for you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Pekné! A bývajú vaši rodičia ešte doma?
exchanges.translation: Nice! And do your parents still live back home?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if your parents still live in your home country. Pick whichever is true for you.

choices.text: Moji rodičia bývajú v inej krajine.
choices.translation: My parents live in another country.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct grammar. Rodičia = parents, bývajú = they live, doma = at home.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Moji rodičia bývajú... — pick whichever is true for your own situation.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: To je super, teraz vás poznám lepšie. Ďakujem, Kai!
exchanges.translation: That's great, now I know you better. Thank you, Kai!
exchanges.prompt: Marek thanks you for sharing. How do you respond warmly?

choices.translation: Thank you too! Nice to meet you.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem aj vám! Teší ma. — warm and natural. You returned the thanks and used the phrase from when you met.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ďakujem aj vám! Teší ma is the warmest natural reply here — returning thanks and closing the introduction nicely.

completionMessage: 🎉 Excellent! You introduced yourself, described how you look, and talked about your family — all in Slovak. Marek knows you much better now.
